spec_match.rationale: The visible type ramp now uses CSS clamp(), cqw units, registered length custom properties, and typed length division in the browser's CSS engine.

interactive.rationale: The sliders update CSS custom-property inputs and the preview container width, while computed-style readouts confirm CSS produced the live font sizes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page copy now frames the demo around typed length division and container-query fluid typography, matching the rendered behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo feature-detects typed calc support, keeps a CSS min-size fallback, and prevents an inverted min/max font-size range.

references.rationale: The page includes the mandatory ChromeStatus link and the CSS Values and Units Level 4 type-checking reference.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared design tokens for backgrounds, borders, accents, and status badges with no raw color literals.

summary: The fluid typography concept now demonstrates native CSS typed arithmetic: JavaScript only updates typed custom-property inputs and labels, while the browser computes the visible ramp with cqw-based clamp() formulas.
